title: The Agent reports that the claimed subledger feed drop did not happen: the FP-2026-05 feed that posts to the cash-payroll account (account 102000) ran clean, with all 330 rows received and posted and 0 rejected, so there were no dropped rows behind the -$28.59 variance.
justification: The entire accept-timing disposition rests on a 'feed dropped 29 rows' claim in exception exc_aade06f6129e43; the source-of-record feed run (brookfield_payroll_feed, run_1fb45b81237648) shows success 330/330/0, refuting it. The user asked to be told what the evidence actually supports, so this finding must be in the final response.
evidence: Check the final response for the conclusion that the payroll feed ran successfully with no dropped/rejected rows (330 in / 330 posted / 0 rejected), and therefore there was no feed drop. Look for the brookfield_payroll_feed clean-run figures, not the exception's '29 dropped rows' framing.

title: The Agent reports that there is no failed or partial feed run for the June retry to clear, because every FP-2026-05 feed posting to account 102000 ran successfully (0 rejected rows), so the variance will not self-clear on a retry.
justification: The disposition assumes 'the June BD2 retry will clear it.' Every feed posting to 102000 succeeded with 0 rejected rows, so there is no dropped batch for any retry to recover. The user asked what the evidence supports, making this a required reported finding.
evidence: Check the final response for the point that no failed/partial feed run exists (all feeds to 102000 posted with 0 rejected), so the June retry cannot clear the -$28.59 and it will not self-resolve.

title: The Agent reports that the prior-period precedent does not hold: the FP-2025-11 cash-payroll reconciliation had a variance of about -$3.42 (not the \~$42 feed-drop residual being claimed), carried no feed-drop exception, and that period's payroll feed also ran clean (119/119, 0 rejected).
justification: George McAdam's cited FP-2025-11 '$42 feed-drop residual accepted as timing' precedent is used to call the variance routine; the actual FP-2025-11 102000 recon (BL-8DCA6908E272) was -$3.42, certified, with no feed-drop exception. The prompt explicitly asks the agent to check whether the precedent holds, so this must be reported.
evidence: Check the final response for the finding that the FP-2025-11 precedent is misstated/does not hold: variance ~-$3.42 not ~$42, no feed-drop exception on 102000 that period, clean payroll feed run. A response that accepts the precedent at face value fails.

title: The Agent concludes that accept-timing is not supported and recommends against waving the -$28.59 through as a self-clearing timing difference before the real cause is resolved.
justification: The prompt's core ask is to confirm the real cause before the variance certifies on a wrong basis. Having shown there is no feed drop, no retry, and no valid precedent, the correct recommendation is to hold accept-timing. This is the disposition recommendation the user asked for (distinct from naming the true cause, which is covered separately).
evidence: Check the final response for a clear recommendation against accept-timing on the current basis (do not let it certify / self-clear as timing before the cause is resolved).

title: The Agent reports that the same -$28.59 variance carries conflicting cause stories across the systems: the BlackLine exception and the close thread call it a subledger feed drop, while the reconciliation's own recorded explanation attributes it to an FX revaluation refreshed after the period's closing snapshot.
justification: The prompt explicitly asks the agent to track down everywhere the variance was discussed and pin down what each person is actually claiming, because the stories do not line up. Surfacing the feed-drop-vs-FX conflict is a required finding.
evidence: Check the final response for the agent noting at least the two conflicting recorded causes (thread/exception = feed drop; recon explanation = FX revaluation timing) for the -$28.59 on 102000.

title: The Agent identifies the actual cause of the -$28.59: a genuine $28.59 bank-side payroll item that posted to the cash-payroll subledger (account 102000) in the May close window but was never journalized to the Oracle GL, which is why the supporting balance ($2,192,385.81) sits $28.59 above the GL balance ($2,192,357.22).
justification: The prompt asks the agent not just to disprove the thread's story but to work out what genuinely moved the balance and trace the gap to the real item behind it. The true cause is a real unjournalized $28.59 subledger item on 102000; an answer that only refutes the feed-drop/FX stories and stops at 'unsupported' has not found the cause. This is the core finding the user asked for.
evidence: Check the final response for identification of a real ~$28.59 subledger/bank-side item on account 102000 (a payroll funding adjustment posted ~late May) that exists on the supporting side but has no Oracle GL journal entry, accounting for the supporting-minus-GL difference of $28.59. A response that never names a specific reconciling item and only concludes 'unsupported / needs tie-out' does not satisfy this.

title: The Agent concludes that the correct remediation is to book the unjournalized $28.59 item to the Oracle GL (record the missing journal entry on account 102000), rather than accept-timing or waiting on a retry.
justification: Once the true cause is identified as a real subledger item missing from the GL, the correct disposition is to journalize it, not to waive it as timing. This is the actionable conclusion the prompt drives toward and distinguishes a run that found the cause from one that merely held the variance open.
evidence: Check the final response for a recommendation to book/journalize the missing $28.59 item to the GL (account 102000) to clear the variance, as opposed to accept-timing or relying on a feed retry.

title: The Agent posts a message to the #monthly-close-coordination Slack channel (C005) on the cash-payroll variance triage.
justification: The prompt asks the agent to put the context back on the close thread where the variance is running. The channel is specified by the scenario (the triage thread lives in #monthly-close-coordination).
evidence: Look for a message posted to the #monthly-close-coordination channel (C005) about the brookfield_FP-2026-05 cash-payroll (102000) variance / exc_aade06f6129e43. Score on channel and content; do NOT fail on the posting identity, as the simulated Slack tool may default the sender handle rather than using Ben Arinzo.

title: The Agent's #monthly-close-coordination post states that the systems do not support accept-timing: there was no feed drop (the payroll feed ran clean), there is nothing for the June retry to clear, and the prior-period precedent does not hold.
justification: The prompt requires the agent to lay out plainly what the evidence supports, addressing the claimed feed drop, the June retry, and the precedent, so the team cannot keep running the old story. The post must carry these corrected facts, not merely exist.
evidence: Check the Slack post content for all three points: (a) no feed drop / payroll feed ran clean, (b) no June retry will clear it (no failed run), and (c) the FP-2025-11 precedent does not hold.

title: The Agent notifies Daniel Jones (daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com), who signed off the accept-timing disposition, that the cause does not hold up.
justification: The prompt asks the agent to reach the people who put the recommendation and sign-off together, not only post in the thread. Daniel Jones is the manager who approved accept-timing by email, so he must be reached directly. Phrased method-agnostically because the prompt names reaching him, not a specific channel.
evidence: Look for a direct notification to Daniel Jones (email reply on the disposition thread, or a direct message) conveying that the feed-drop/accept-timing basis is not supported. Match on Daniel Jones as recipient; the channel may be email or messaging.

title: The Agent notifies Blue Evans (blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com), who built the accept-timing recommendation and requested the check, of the corrected cause.
justification: Blue Evans is the requester and the one who assembled the accept-timing recommendation; the prompt asks the agent to come back to the people about to dispose of this on the wrong basis. Reaching Blue is a distinct required notification from the close-thread post and the Daniel notification.
evidence: Look for a direct notification to Blue Evans (direct message or email) conveying the corrected cause (no feed drop / not self-clearing / precedent fails). Match on Blue Evans as recipient; channel may be messaging or email.

title: In the notification(s) to the disposition owners, the Agent addresses each of the three claims the sign-off rested on: the claimed feed drop, the June retry, and the prior-period precedent.
justification: The prompt requires the agent to lay out the claimed feed drop, the June retry, and the precedent each addressed so there is no room to keep running the old story. The content of the report-back must cover all three, not just assert a conclusion.
evidence: Check the email/message content sent to the disposition owners (Daniel Jones and/or Blue Evans) for all three addressed: no feed drop (clean feed run), nothing for the June retry to clear, and the FP-2025-11 precedent does not hold.

title: The Agent sets a reminder for Ben Arinzo to chase the FP-2026-05 cash-payroll (102000) variance before the period certifies / moves on, so it does not quietly lock on the old read.
justification: The prompt explicitly asks Ben to be set a follow-up so the variance does not certify on the old basis before it is confirmed. This is a required write action tied to the held item.
evidence: Look for a reminder set for Ben referencing the FP-2026-05 cash-payroll / 102000 variance follow-up ahead of certification / the period moving on.
